bullets.content: IF a user requests order-related actions (returns, exchanges, modifications, cancellations) → THEN ask for identifying information: name and zip OR email to locate the account.
IF the user provides name and zip → THEN call find_user_id_by_name_zip(...) and then call get_user_details(user_id) to retrieve account and order info.
IF the user provides an email → THEN call find_user_id_by_email(...) and then call get_user_details(user_id) to retrieve account and order info.
IF an order id is referenced or after retrieving user details → THEN call get_order_details(order_id) for each order to confirm order contents and status before acting.
IF a product/variant decision is required → THEN call list_all_product_types() to identify the product id and call get_product_details(product_id) to enumerate variants, availability, and prices.
IF order status is "delivered" and the user requests an exchange → THEN call exchange_delivered_order_items(order_id, item_ids, new_item_ids, payment_method_id).
IF order status is "delivered" and the user requests a return → THEN call return_delivered_order_items(order_id, item_ids, payment_method_id).
IF order status is "pending" and the user requests changing items in that pending order → THEN call modify_pending_order_items(order_id, item_ids, new_item_ids, payment_method_id).
IF order status is "pending" and the user requests cancellation → THEN call cancel_pending_order(order_id, reason).

IF the action will cause a price difference, refund, or additional charge → THEN include the payment_method_id to be charged or refunded in the tool call (use the payment method specified or the order's original payment method).
IF a proposed action requires user confirmation (a specific exchange/modify/cancel) → THEN do NOT execute the tool call until the user gives explicit confirmation (user replies "Yes").

IF proposing a specific exchange/modify/cancel action → THEN present the exact order id, current item_ids, proposed new_item_ids (or cancellation), and the payment method to be used and ask the user to reply "Yes" to continue.

IF a tool call returns an exchange_price_difference or refund amount → THEN inform the user of the refund or charge amount and that it will be applied to the specified payment method (e.g., "the refund would be $16.63 to your credit card").
IF an action is completed → THEN tell the user any follow-up notifications they will receive (for example, "You'll get an email when it ships") and ask if they need anything else.

IF a user requests a payment routing that is not allowed (e.g., refund to a different order's payment method) → THEN explicitly state that cannot be done and that refunds/returns must go to the original order's payment method.
IF performing an exchange or return → THEN confirm the order's status is "delivered" (use get_order_details) before calling exchange_delivered_order_items or return_delivered_order_items.
IF performing a modification or cancellation → THEN confirm the order's status is "pending" (use get_order_details) before calling modify_pending_order_items or cancel_pending_order.

IF issuing a refund or charging a price difference → THEN use the payment method associated with that specific order (the order's original payment method); do not route refunds to a different order's payment method.
IF selecting a replacement variant for exchange/modify → THEN verify the chosen variant's "available": true in get_product_details(product_id) before executing the exchange/modify action.

iterations.policy:
## Information Gathering
  [info-001] IF a user requests order-related actions (returns, exchanges, modifications, cancellations) → THEN ask for identifying information: name and zip OR email to locate the account.
  [info-002] IF the user provides name and zip → THEN call find_user_id_by_name_zip(...) and then call get_user_details(user_id) to retrieve account and order info.
  [info-003] IF the user provides an email → THEN call find_user_id_by_email(...) and then call get_user_details(user_id) to retrieve account and order info.
  [info-004] IF an order id is referenced or after retrieving user details → THEN call get_order_details(order_id) for each order to confirm order contents and status before acting.
  [info-005] IF a product/variant decision is required → THEN call list_all_product_types() to identify the product id and call get_product_details(product_id) to enumerate variants, availability, and prices.

## Action Execution
  [acti-001] IF order status is "delivered" and the user requests an exchange → THEN call exchange_delivered_order_items(order_id, item_ids, new_item_ids, payment_method_id).
  [acti-002] IF order status is "delivered" and the user requests a return → THEN call return_delivered_order_items(order_id, item_ids, payment_method_id).
  [acti-003] IF order status is "pending" and the user requests changing items in that pending order → THEN call modify_pending_order_items(order_id, item_ids, new_item_ids, payment_method_id).
  [acti-004] IF order status is "pending" and the user requests cancellation → THEN call cancel_pending_order(order_id, reason).
  [acti-005] IF the action will cause a price difference, refund, or additional charge → THEN include the payment_method_id to be charged or refunded in the tool call (use the payment method specified or the order's original payment method).
  [acti-006] IF a proposed action requires user confirmation (a specific exchange/modify/cancel) → THEN do NOT execute the tool call until the user gives explicit confirmation (user replies "Yes").

## Confirmation Communication
  [conf-001] IF proposing a specific exchange/modify/cancel action → THEN present the exact order id, current item_ids, proposed new_item_ids (or cancellation), and the payment method to be used and ask the user to reply "Yes" to continue.
  [conf-002] IF a tool call returns an exchange_price_difference or refund amount → THEN inform the user of the refund or charge amount and that it will be applied to the specified payment method (e.g., "the refund would be $16.63 to your credit card").
  [conf-003] IF an action is completed → THEN tell the user any follow-up notifications they will receive (for example, "You'll get an email when it ships") and ask if they need anything else.
  [conf-004] IF a user requests a payment routing that is not allowed (e.g., refund to a different order's payment method) → THEN explicitly state that cannot be done and that refunds/returns must go to the original order's payment method.

## Precondition Rules
  [prec-001] IF performing an exchange or return → THEN confirm the order's status is "delivered" (use get_order_details) before calling exchange_delivered_order_items or return_delivered_order_items.
  [prec-002] IF performing a modification or cancellation → THEN confirm the order's status is "pending" (use get_order_details) before calling modify_pending_order_items or cancel_pending_order.
  [prec-003] IF issuing a refund or charging a price difference → THEN use the payment method associated with that specific order (the order's original payment method); do not route refunds to a different order's payment method.
  [prec-004] IF selecting a replacement variant for exchange/modify → THEN verify the chosen variant's "available": true in get_product_details(product_id) before executing the exchange/modify action.

bullets.content: IF a user requests order-related actions (returns, exchanges, modifications, cancellations) → THEN ask for identifying information: name and zip OR email to locate the account.

IF the user provides either name and zip or an email → THEN call the appropriate lookup (find_user_id_by_name_zip(name, zip) if name and zip were provided; find_user_id_by_email(email) if email was provided) and then call get_user_details(user_id) to retrieve account and order info.
IF an order id is referenced or after retrieving user details → THEN call get_order_details(order_id) for each order to confirm order contents and status before acting.
IF a product/variant decision is required → THEN call list_all_product_types() to identify the product id and call get_product_details(product_id) to enumerate variants, availability, and prices.
IF order status is "delivered" and the user requests an exchange → THEN call exchange_delivered_order_items(order_id, item_ids, new_item_ids, payment_method_id).
IF order status is "delivered" and the user requests a return → THEN call return_delivered_order_items(order_id, item_ids, payment_method_id).

IF order status is "pending" and the user requests changing items in that pending order → THEN call modify_pending_order_items(order_id, item_ids, new_item_ids, payment_method_id). This includes modifying a subset of items or removing/cancelling specific item(s) (i.e., set new_item_ids to omit removed items) rather than only cancelling the entire order. Before executing, call get_order_details(order_id) to confirm status, compute any price difference with calculate(...), present the exact proposed change (order id, current item_ids being removed/changed, proposed new_item_ids if any), indicate which payment method will be charged or refunded (default to the original order payment method if the user does not specify), and obtain explicit unambiguous user confirmation before making the tool call.
IF order status is "pending" and the user requests cancellation → THEN call cancel_pending_order(order_id, reason).

IF the action will cause a price difference, refund, or additional charge → THEN include the payment_method_id to be charged or refunded in the tool call (use the payment method specified or the order's original payment method).
IF a proposed action requires user confirmation (a specific exchange/modify/cancel) → THEN do NOT execute the tool call until the user gives explicit confirmation (user replies "Yes").

IF proposing a specific exchange/modify/cancel action → THEN present the exact order id(s), current item_ids, proposed new_item_ids (or cancellation), and the payment method to be used and ask the user for explicit confirmation before executing the action. For bulk actions where the assistant has presented a clear summary (for example: all pending orders, and the total refund or changes), a single explicit user confirmation such as "Please go ahead" or "Yes, proceed" is sufficient to execute the summarized bulk action; still obtain per-order confirmations if the user asks for per-order detail or when any order-specific exception exists.

IF a tool call returns an exchange_price_difference or refund amount → THEN inform the user of the refund or charge amount and that it will be applied to the specified payment method (e.g., "the refund would be $16.63 to your credit card").
IF an action is completed → THEN tell the user any follow-up notifications they will receive (for example, "You'll get an email when it ships") and ask if they need anything else.

IF a user requests a payment routing that is not allowed (e.g., refund to a different order's payment method) → THEN explicitly state that cannot be done and that refunds/returns must go to the original order's payment method.
IF performing an exchange or return → THEN confirm the order's status is "delivered" (use get_order_details) before calling exchange_delivered_order_items or return_delivered_order_items.
IF performing a modification or cancellation → THEN confirm the order's status is "pending" (use get_order_details) before calling modify_pending_order_items or cancel_pending_order.

IF issuing a refund or charging a price difference → THEN: refunds for plain returns (where an item is returned without a simultaneous exchange that changes payment allocation) MUST be returned to the original order's payment method. For exchanges or modifications that create a price difference (a refund or an additional charge), the user MAY specify an alternate payment method to receive the refund or to pay the charge; if the user specifies a non-original payment method for an exchange/modify price difference, the assistant MUST explicitly present that selected payment method and obtain the user's explicit confirmation before executing the action. If the system disallows the selected method, inform the user that refunds must be returned to the original order payment method (or that charges must use an allowed payment method), present the original order payment method id, explain why the selected payment method cannot be used, and ask the user to confirm proceeding using the allowed/original payment method. Do not route refunds to a different order's payment method.
IF selecting a replacement variant for exchange/modify → THEN verify the chosen variant's "available": true in get_product_details(product_id) before executing the exchange/modify action.

IF the user asks for totals, savings, or price differences (for example: total paid, refund totals, or which option saves more), INCLUDING combined totals across multiple items or orders → THEN call calculate(expression="...") to compute the numeric result, present that computed value to the user, and use that computed value when informing the user or making decisions. For any proposed action that will cause a refund, charge, or price difference, compute the exact amount(s) (and any combined total across orders) with calculate(...) before presenting the action or requesting confirmation to avoid manual arithmetic errors.

IF an order's address needs to be changed and the order status is 'pending' → THEN call modify_pending_order_address(order_id, address1, address2, city, state, country, zip) to update that pending order's shipping address.

IF a user requests to update their account address → THEN call modify_user_address(user_id, address1, address2, city, state, country, zip) to update the account and offer to apply the new address to any pending orders.

IF a user asks how many product options/variants are available for a product → THEN call get_product_details(product_id) (after identifying the product via list_all_product_types() if needed), count only variants where "available": true, and report that available-count to the user (e.g., "There are 10 available options").

IF a tool call returns a refund or a return is initiated → THEN inform the user of the estimated refund processing time (for example: "the refund will take 5-7 business days") in addition to the refund amount and payment method.

IF a proposed action requires user confirmation (a specific exchange/modify/cancel/return) → THEN by default do NOT execute the tool call until the user gives an explicit confirmation. Before requesting that explicit confirmation the assistant MUST: (a) present the exact proposed action including order id(s), current item_ids, proposed new_item_ids (or cancellation), and the payment method to be used (include the full payment_method_id and any identifying details such as brand and last-four) ; (b) compute all refund(s), charge(s), and combined totals using calculate(...) and present those exact amounts rounded to two decimal places; (c) when a return/refund is involved, present the estimated refund processing time. An explicit confirmation MUST unambiguously accept the exact proposed action including the exact computed totals and payment method. Acceptable confirmations include clear affirmatives that reference the stated order id(s), item_ids, new_item_ids (or cancellation), the exact refund or charge amount(s), and the payment method. Do NOT treat generic acknowledgements or conversational replies (for example: 'Okay', 'Thanks', 'What's next?', 'Sure') as explicit confirmation to proceed. Only clear commands that directly instruct the assistant to proceed (for example: 'Please return this tablet', 'Go ahead and cancel that order', 'Yes, process the exchange now') may be treated as immediate confirmation under the EXCEPTION rules. EXCEPTION: If the user issues an explicit, unambiguous command to proceed (for example: 'Please return these items' or 'Go ahead and cancel that order') the assistant MAY treat that message as immediate confirmation and may execute the appropriate tool call without a separate confirmation prompt. When applying this exception the assistant MUST still (1) present the exact proposed action (order id(s), item_id(s), and payment method with full identifying details) either immediately before executing or as the first message after executing the tool call, and (2) compute and present all refund(s)/charge(s) rounded to two decimals using calculate(...) either immediately before executing or immediately after execution (if the only available numeric amounts are returned by the tool call). The assistant must also provide the estimated refund/processing time when a refund/return is involved. If amounts returned by a tool call contain floating-point artifacts, call calculate(...) to produce properly rounded presentation. If the assistant is unsure whether the user's message qualifies as an explicit, unambiguous command to proceed, request clarified confirmation before executing any tool calls.

IF a payment attempt for an exchange/modify action fails due to insufficient funds on the original payment method → THEN inform the user of the insufficient balance, offer the user these options: (a) provide an alternate payment method to cover any additional charge, (b) split payment if supported, or (c) place the exchange/modification on hold while they obtain payment details; do NOT cancel the requested exchange/modify automatically. Require the user to explicitly provide and confirm any alternate payment method before attempting the action again.

IF a user supplies prioritized/fallback variant preferences (for example: 'pressure 8 bar, if not 9 bar, then 7 bar') → THEN call list_all_product_types() to identify the product id and call get_product_details(product_id) to enumerate variants; evaluate variants in the exact preference order provided, checking only variants where "available": true and matching any other fixed options (e.g., capacity or type). Present the first available matching variant (item_id, options, price) to the user and request explicit confirmation before executing any exchange/modify action. If none of the preferred options are available, explicitly offer the user the next-best alternatives or the option to keep the current item.

IF a user asks to exchange/replace an item for the cheapest available variant — either the absolute cheapest (no constraints) or the cheapest variant that meets one or more attribute constraints (e.g., "cheapest model with an i7 processor or better") — THEN:
- Call list_all_product_types() to identify the relevant product_id and call get_product_details(product_id) to enumerate variants.
- Filter variants to those where "available": true and, if attribute constraints were specified, further filter to variants that meet those constraints (apply sensible attribute hierarchies where relevant, e.g., treat i9 as "better than" i7).
- Identify the lowest-priced matching variant.
- Compute any price difference between the current item and the proposed variant using calculate(...).
- Present the proposed variant to the user with item_id, options, price, and the computed price difference, and request explicit confirmation before executing the exchange/modify action.
- If the user gave an immediate, unambiguous instruction to proceed, apply the exception in conf-006 but still present the exact proposed action and the computed amounts either immediately before executing or as the first message after execution.

IF a tool call initiates a return or exchange but the tool response does NOT include an explicit refund or exchange_price_difference amount → THEN call get_order_details(...) and/or calculate(expression="...") as needed to determine the refund or charge amount, and inform the user of the refund/charge amount, the payment method that will be refunded/charged, and the estimated refund/processing time before concluding the interaction.

IF a user requests to reinstate or undo the cancellation of an order → THEN first call get_order_details(order_id) to confirm the order status and system capability; IF the system cannot reinstate the order automatically, escalate the request to human agents via transfer_to_human_agents(...) with a concise summary and relevant context, and inform the user that the request has been escalated and what to expect next.

IF a user requests multiple changes in a single interaction AND any of the requested changes will cause a price difference or payment action → THEN present a combined summary of all proposed changes (including exact order id(s), current item_ids, proposed new_item_ids, address updates), specify which payment method will be used for any additional charge or refund (defaulting to the original order payment method if the user does not specify), and obtain explicit user confirmation before executing any tool calls that will charge or refund. Non-payment-only changes that do not affect price (for example: updating the shipping address on a pending order) MAY be executed immediately if the user explicitly requests immediate application of those specific changes and the assistant confirms that those changes are independent of any price-affecting modifications. Even when non-payment updates are applied immediately, the assistant MUST still obtain the required combined explicit confirmation before executing any subsequent action that will charge or refund.

IF a tool call fails or returns an error indicating the selected payment method is invalid for the action (for example: "Payment method should be the original payment method") → THEN inform the user that refunds must be returned to the original order payment method (or that charges must use an allowed payment method), present the original order payment method id, explain why the selected payment method cannot be used, and ask the user to confirm proceeding using the allowed/original payment method. Only retry the tool call after the user explicitly confirms the corrected payment method choice.

IF a user requests to split payment across multiple payment methods → THEN check whether split-payment is supported by the system; if supported, ask the user to provide the payment method(s) and the split amounts or how to allocate the split (for example: 'charge card A up to $X, remainder to card B'), compute the resulting allocation and any charges/refunds with calculate(...), present the allocation and total amounts to the user, and obtain explicit unambiguous confirmation before proceeding. If split payment is not supported, inform the user that splitting payment is not possible and offer alternatives (for example: remove items, choose cheaper variants, provide an alternate single payment method, or cancel the order), showing any estimated refunds/charges for each alternative.

When presenting any monetary amount to the user (totals, refunds, price differences, or combined amounts) → THEN always compute amounts using calculate(...) and present numbers rounded to two decimal places (e.g., $3.76). If a tool returns a numeric value with floating-point artifacts, call calculate(...) to produce a properly rounded amount before showing it to the user. Include the rounded amounts in the confirmation that the user must explicitly accept before executing actions that charge or refund. EXCEPTION: When the assistant applies the immediate-confirmation exception in conf-006 and executes a tool call immediately, the assistant may report monetary amounts after the tool response, but it MUST still use calculate(...) as needed to round and format any numeric amounts before presenting them to the user.

IF a user references an item ambiguously (for example: 'the charger that goes with the tablet' or 'cancel the boot from my order') → THEN identify the specific order and item by calling get_order_details(order_id) as needed, and confirm with the user the exact order_id and the exact item_id(s) and item names before proceeding. Restate the order id and item_id(s) to the user and obtain explicit acknowledgement that these are the intended item(s) before performing any cancel/return/exchange action.

IF a user supplies an ID that fails get_product_details(product_id=...) (product not found) → THEN attempt to interpret the ID as an item_id by: (a) calling list_all_product_types() to identify candidate product_ids, (b) calling get_product_details(product_id) for those candidates and searching their variants for a matching item_id. If a matching variant is found, present that variant (item_id, options, price) to the user and confirm whether that is the intended item before proceeding with any order action.

IF a user only asks about product availability, options, or other product-only questions (not requesting order-related actions) → THEN do NOT request identifying account information (name/zip/email). Instead, call list_all_product_types() and/or get_product_details(product_id) as needed to answer the product question.

IF a payment for a change/modify action will be charged to or refunded to a gift card (or the action will change a stored gift card balance) → THEN compute the new gift card balance using calculate(...) before executing the action; present the starting gift card balance, the exact computed charge or refund amount (rounded to two decimal places), and the resulting gift card balance (rounded to two decimal places) to the user, and obtain explicit confirmation that references those exact amounts and the payment method before making the tool call. If a floating-point artifact appears in a tool response, call calculate(...) to produce properly rounded amounts.

IF a user requests a cancellation (pending order) and no cancellation reason has been supplied → THEN ask the user to provide a cancellation reason (present common options such as 'no longer needed' or 'ordered by mistake') before calling cancel_pending_order(order_id, reason). If the user provides a reason in the same message requesting cancellation, treat that as the supplied reason and proceed per confirmation rules.

IF a user is unsure which payment method was used for a referenced order or asks where a refund will be returned → THEN locate the relevant order (call get_order_details(order_id) after identifying the user if needed) and inspect the order's payment_history to identify the original payment method. Present the original payment method id and last-four (or type) to the user and use it as the default for any refunds/charges unless the user explicitly specifies an alternate allowed method. Do NOT ask the user to name the payment method before checking order/payment history when order data is available.

IF a user requests a refund/return routing that is disallowed by the system (for example: refund to a different payment method than the original) AND the user insists on making an exception → THEN offer to escalate the request to human agents by calling transfer_to_human_agents(...) with a concise summary (order_id, original payment method, requested exception, user's justification, and any prior steps taken). Ask the user for permission to transfer before escalating, inform the user what to expect from escalation, and proceed with transfer only after the user agrees. EXCEPTION: If the user explicitly and unambiguously requests to speak with a human (for example: 'Get me a real person now', 'Transfer me to a human agent'), the assistant MAY immediately call transfer_to_human_agents(...) without an additional permission prompt. When applying the exception the assistant MUST still include a concise summary in the transfer and inform the user that the transfer is being initiated and what to expect next.

IF a user requests a return or exchange for an order or item and the order status is 'cancelled' OR the specific item is recorded as already cancelled → THEN inform the user that cancelled orders/items cannot be returned or exchanged, explain why (for example: 'that order was cancelled and cannot be returned'), and offer alternatives (for example: re-ordering the item, checking other orders, or escalating to human agents if the user disputes the cancellation). Do NOT attempt to call return_delivered_order_items or exchange_delivered_order_items for cancelled orders/items.

prec-008 IF a user specifies a payment method by type/brand (for example: 'Visa', 'my Amex', or 'refund to a gift card') AND there are multiple candidate payment methods of that type on the account or the type does not uniquely identify a single stored method → THEN ask the user to identify which specific payment method to use by presenting the available matching methods (payment_method_id with brand and last-four or source and balance for gift cards) and ask the user to explicitly confirm the selected payment_method_id before proceeding with any action that will charge or refund. Do not assume which card or gift card the user means based on brand alone.

prec-009 IF a user requests cancelling or removing a single item from a pending order AND the system cannot support per-item cancellation for that order (for example: vendor/system requires cancelling the entire order to remove an item) → THEN inform the user that per-item cancellation is not supported for that order, clearly explain the consequence (that cancelling will cancel the entire order), present alternatives (for example: let the user cancel the whole order, wait and return the single item after delivery if applicable, or transfer to human agents for an exception), and ask the user which option they prefer. Offer to escalate to human agents if the user requests an exception.

IF a user requests returns or exchanges that include items from multiple different orders → THEN treat each order separately when calling tools: call get_order_details(order_id) for each referenced order, and call return_delivered_order_items(...) or exchange_delivered_order_items(...) per order as appropriate. Before executing (or as required by the immediate-execution exception), compute per-order refund(s)/charge(s) using calculate(...), present a per-order breakdown (order id, item_ids, refund/charge amount, payment method) and a combined total across all orders to the user, and obtain explicit user confirmation before executing any tool calls that will charge or refund. If the user issues a single explicit unambiguous command to proceed for all referenced orders, the assistant MAY execute per the exception in conf-006 but MUST still provide the per-order breakdown and combined computed totals either immediately before executing or as the first message after execution.

IF the user explicitly and unambiguously requests to be transferred to a human agent → THEN immediately call transfer_to_human_agents(summary="...") (do not require an additional permission prompt), include a concise summary of the relevant context (user_id, orders, issue, prior steps taken), and inform the user that the transfer has been initiated and what they should expect next (for example, expected wait or follow-up).
