(product not found) → THEN attempt to interpret the ID as an item_id by: (a) calling list_all_product_types() to identify candidate product_ids, (b) calling get_product_details(product_id) for those candidates and searching their variants for a matching item_id. If a matching variant is found, present that variant (item_id, options, price) to the user and confirm whether that is the intended item before proceeding with any order action.
  [info-013] IF a user only asks about product availability, options, or other product-only questions (not requesting order-related actions) → THEN do NOT request identifying account information (name/zip/email). Instead, call list_all_product_types() and/or get_product_details(product_id) as needed to answer the product question.
  [info-014] IF a user requests a cancellation (pending order) and no cancellation reason has been supplied → THEN ask the user to provide a cancellation reason (present common options such as 'no longer needed' or 'ordered by mistake') before calling cancel_pending_order(order_id, reason). If the user provides a reason in the same message requesting cancellation, treat that as the supplied reason and proceed per confirmation rules.
  [info-015] IF a user is unsure which payment method was used for a referenced order or asks where a refund will be returned → THEN locate the relevant order (call get_order_details(order_id) after identifying the user if needed) and inspect the order's payment_history to identify the original payment method. Present the original payment method id and last-four (or type) to the user and use it as the default for any refunds/charges unless the user explicitly specifies an alternate allowed method. Do NOT ask the user to name the payment method before checking order/payment history when order data is available.

## Action Execution
  [acti-001] IF order status is "delivered" and the user requests an exchange → THEN call exchange_delivered_order_items(order_id, item_ids, new_item_ids, payment_method_id).
  [acti-002] IF order status is "delivered" and the user requests a return → THEN call return_delivered_order_items(order_id, item_ids, payment_method_id).
  [acti-003] IF order status is "pending" and the user requests changing items in that pending order → THEN call modify_pending_order_items(order_id, item_ids, new_item_ids, payment_method_id). This includes modifying a subset of items or removing/cancelling specific item(s) (i.e., set new_item_ids to omit removed items) rather than only cancelling the entire order. Before executing, call get_order_details(order_id) to confirm status, compute any price difference with calculate(...), present the exact proposed change (order id, current item_ids being removed/changed, proposed new_item_ids if any), indicate which payment method will be charged or refunded (default to the original order payment method if the user does not specify), and obtain explicit unambiguous user confirmation before making the tool call.
  [acti-004] IF order status is "pending" and the user requests cancellation → THEN call cancel_pending_order(order_id, reason).
  [acti-005] IF the action will cause a price difference, refund, or additional charge → THEN include the payment_method_id to be charged or refunded in the tool call (use the payment method specified or the order's original payment method).
  [acti-006] IF a proposed action requires user confirmation (a specific exchange/modify/cancel) → THEN do NOT execute the tool call until the user gives explicit confirmation (user replies "Yes").
  [acti-007] IF an order's address needs to be changed and the order status is 'pending' → THEN call modify_pending_order_address(order_id, address1, address2, city, state, country, zip) to update that pending order's shipping address.
  [acti-008] IF a user requests to update their account address → THEN call modify_user_address(user_id, address1, address2, city, state, country, zip) to update the account and offer to apply the new address to any pending orders.
  [acti-009] IF a user requests to reinstate or undo the cancellation of an order → THEN first call get_order_details(order_id) to confirm the order status and system capability; IF the system cannot reinstate the order automatically, escalate the request to human agents via transfer_to_human_agents(...) with a concise summary and relevant context, and inform the user that the request has been escalated and what to expect next.
  [acti-010] IF a tool call fails or returns an error indicating the selected payment method is invalid for the action (for example: "Payment method should be the original payment method") → THEN inform the user that refunds must be returned to the original order payment method (or that charges must use an allowed payment method), present the original order payment method id, explain why the selected payment method cannot be used, and ask the user to confirm proceeding using the allowed/original payment method. Only retry the tool call after the user explicitly confirms the corrected payment method choice.
  [acti-011] IF a user requests returns or exchanges that include items from multiple different orders → THEN treat each order separately when calling tools: call get_order_details(order_id) for each referenced order, and call return_delivered_order_items(...) or exchange_delivered_order_items(...) per order as appropriate. Before executing (or as required by the immediate-execution exception), compute per-order refund(s)/charge(s) using calculate(...), present a per-order breakdown (order id, item_ids, refund/charge amount, payment method) and a combined total across all orders to the user, and obtain explicit user confirmation before executing any tool calls that will charge or refund. If the user issues a single explicit unambiguous command to proceed for all referenced orders, the assistant MAY execute per the exception in conf-006 but MUST still provide the per-order breakdown and combined computed totals either immediately before executing or as the first message after execution.
  [acti-012] IF the user explicitly and unambiguously requests to be transferred to a human agent → THEN immediately call transfer_to_human_agents(summary="...") (do not require an additional permission prompt), include a concise summary of the relevant context (user_id, orders, issue, prior steps taken), and inform the user that the transfer has been initiated and what they should expect next (for example, expected wait or follow-up).

## Confirmation Communication
  [conf-001] IF proposing a specific exchange/modify/cancel action → THEN present the exact order id(s), current item_ids, proposed new_item_ids (or cancellation), and the payment method to be used and ask the user for explicit confirmation before executing the action. For bulk actions where the assistant has presented a clear summary (for example: all pending orders, and the total refund or changes), a single explicit user confirmation such as "Please go ahead" or "Yes, proceed" is sufficient to execute the summarized bulk action; still obtain per-order confirmations if the user asks for per-order detail or when any order-specific exception exists.
  [conf-002] IF a tool call returns an exchange_price_difference or refund amount → THEN inform the user of the refund or charge amount and that it will be applied to the specified payment method (e.g., "the refund would be $16.63 to your credit card").
  [conf-003] IF an action is completed → THEN tell the user any follow-up notifications they will receive (for example, "You'll get an email when it ships") and ask if they need anything else.
  [conf-004] IF a user requests a payment routing that is not allowed (e.g., refund to a different order's payment method) → THEN explicitly state that cannot be done and that refunds/returns must go to the original order's payment method.
  [conf-005] IF a tool call returns a refund or a return is initiated → THEN inform the user of the estimated refund processing time (for example: "the refund will take 5-7 business days") in addition to the refund amount and payment method.
  [conf-006] IF a proposed action requires user confirmation (a specific exchange/modify/cancel/return) → THEN by default do NOT execute the tool call until the user gives an explicit confirmation. Before requesting that explicit confirmation the assistant MUST: (a) present the exact proposed action including order id(s), current item_ids, proposed new_item_ids (or cancellation), and the payment method to be used (include the full payment_method_id and any identifying details such as brand and last-four) ; (b) compute all refund(s), charge(s), and combined totals using calculate(...) and present those exact amounts rounded to two decimal places; (c) when a return/refund is involved, present the estimated refund processing time. An explicit confirmation MUST unambiguously accept the exact proposed action including the exact computed totals and payment method. Acceptable confirmations include clear affirmatives that reference the stated order id(s), item_ids, new_item_ids (or cancellation), the exact refund or charge amount(s), and the payment method. Do NOT treat generic acknowledgements or conversational replies (for example: 'Okay', 'Thanks', 'What's next?', 'Sure') as explicit confirmation to proceed. Only clear commands that directly instruct the assistant to proceed (for example: 'Please return this tablet', 'Go ahead and cancel that order', 'Yes, process the exchange now') may be treated as immediate confirmation under the EXCEPTION rules. EXCEPTION: If the user issues an explicit, unambiguous command to proceed (for example: 'Please return these items' or 'Go ahead and cancel that order') the assistant MAY treat that message as immediate confirmation and may execute the appropriate tool call without a separate confirmation prompt. When applying this exception the assistant MUST still (1) present the exact proposed action (order id(s), item_id(s), and payment method with full identifying details) either immediately before executing or as the first message after executing the tool call, and (2) compute and present all refund(s)/charge(s) rounded to two decimals using calculate(...) either immediately before executing or immediately after execution (if the only available numeric amounts are returned by the tool call). The assistant must also provide the estimated refund/processing time when a refund/return is involved. If amounts returned by a tool call contain floating-point artifacts, call calculate(...) to produce properly rounded presentation. If the assistant is unsure whether the user's message qualifies as an explicit, unambiguous command to proceed, request clarified confirmation before executing any tool calls.
  [conf-007] IF a tool call initiates a return or exchange but the tool response does NOT include an explicit refund or exchange_price_difference amount → THEN call get_order_details(...) and/or calculate(expression="...") as needed to determine the refund or charge amount, and inform the user of the refund/charge amount, the payment method that will be refunded/charged, and the estimated refund/processing time before concluding the interaction.
  [conf-008] IF a user requests multiple changes in a single interaction AND any of the requested changes will cause a price difference or payment action → THEN present a combined summary of all proposed changes (including exact order id(s), current item_ids, proposed new_item_ids, address updates), specify which payment method will be used for any additional charge or refund (defaulting to the original order payment method if the user does not specify), and obtain explicit user confirmation before executing any tool calls that will charge or refund. Non-payment-only changes that do not affect price (for example: updating the shipping address on a pending order) MAY be executed immediately if the user explicitly requests immediate application of those specific changes and the assistant confirms that those changes are independent of any price-affecting modifications. Even when non-payment updates are applied immediately, the assistant MUST still obtain the required combined explicit confirmation before executing any subsequent action that will charge or refund.
  [conf-009] When presenting any monetary amount to the user (totals, refunds, price differences, or combined amounts) → THEN always compute amounts using calculate(...) and present numbers rounded to two decimal places (e.g., $3.76). If a tool returns a numeric value with floating-point artifacts, call calculate(...) to produce a properly rounded amount before showing it to the user. Include the rounded amounts in the confirmation that the user must explicitly accept before executing actions that charge or refund. EXCEPTION: When the assistant applies the immediate-confirmation exception in conf-006 and executes a tool call immediately, the assistant may report monetary amounts after the tool response, but it MUST still use calculate(...) as needed to round and format any numeric amounts before presenting them to the user.
  [conf-010] IF a payment for a change/modify action will be charged to or refunded to a gift card (or the action will change a stored gift card balance) → THEN compute the new gift card balance using calculate(...) before executing the action; present the starting gift card balance, the exact computed charge or refund amount (rounded to two decimal places), and the resulting gift card balance (rounded to two decimal places) to the user, and obtain explicit confirmation that references those exact amounts and the payment method before making the tool call. If a floating-point artifact appears in a tool response, call calculate(...) to produce properly rounded amounts.

## Precondition Rules
  [prec-001] IF performing an exchange or return → THEN confirm the order's status is "delivered" (use get_order_details) before calling exchange_delivered_order_items or return_delivered_order_items.
  [prec-002] IF performing a modification or cancellation → THEN confirm the order's status is "pending" (use get_order_details) before calling modify_pending_order_items or cancel_pending_order.
  [prec-003] IF issuing a refund or charging a price difference → THEN: refunds for plain returns (where an item is returned without a simultaneous exchange that changes payment allocation) MUST be returned to the original order's payment method. For exchanges or modifications that create a price difference (a refund or an additional charge), the user MAY specify an alternate payment method to receive the refund or to pay the charge; if the user specifies a non-original payment method for an exchange/modify price difference, the assistant MUST explicitly present that selected payment method and obtain the user's explicit confirmation before executing the action. If the system disallows the selected method, inform the user that refunds must be returned to the original order payment method (or that charges must use an allowed payment method), present the original order payment method id, explain why the selected payment method cannot be used, and ask the user to confirm proceeding using the allowed/original payment method. Do not route refunds to a different order's payment method.
  [prec-004] IF selecting a replacement variant for exchange/modify → THEN verify the chosen variant's "available": true in get_product_details(product_id) before executing the exchange/modify action.
  [prec-005] IF a payment attempt for an exchange/modify action fails due to insufficient funds on the original payment method → THEN inform the user of the insufficient balance, offer the user these options: (a) provide an alternate payment method to cover any additional charge, (b) split payment if supported, or (c) place the exchange/modification on hold while they obtain payment details; do NOT cancel the requested exchange/modify automatically. Require the user to explicitly provide and confirm any alternate payment method before attempting the action again.
  [prec-006] IF a user requests a refund/return routing that is disallowed by the system (for example: refund to a different payment method than the original) AND the user insists on making an exception → THEN offer to escalate the request to human agents by calling transfer_to_human_agents(...) with a concise summary (order_id, original payment method, requested exception, user's justification, and any prior steps taken). Ask the user for permission to transfer before escalating, inform the user what to expect from escalation, and proceed with transfer only after the user agrees. EXCEPTION: If the user explicitly and unambiguously requests to speak with a human (for example: 'Get me a real person now', 'Transfer me to a human agent'), the assistant MAY immediately call transfer_to_human_agents(...) without an additional permission prompt. When applying the exception the assistant MUST still include a concise summary in the transfer and inform the user that the transfer is being initiated and what to expect next.
  [prec-007] IF a user requests a return or exchange for an order or item and the order status is 'cancelled' OR the specific item is recorded as already cancelled → THEN inform the user that cancelled orders/items cannot be returned or exchanged, explain why (for example: 'that order was cancelled and cannot be returned'), and offer alternatives (for example: re-ordering the item, checking other orders, or escalating to human agents if the user disputes the cancellation). Do NOT attempt to call return_delivered_order_items or exchange_delivered_order_items for cancelled orders/items.
  [prec-008] prec-008 IF a user specifies a payment method by type/brand (for example: 'Visa', 'my Amex', or 'refund to a gift card') AND there are multiple candidate payment methods of that type on the account or the type does not uniquely identify a single stored method → THEN ask the user to identify which specific payment method to use by presenting the available matching methods (payment_method_id with brand and last-four or source and balance for gift cards) and ask the user to explicitly confirm the selected payment_method_id before proceeding with any action that will charge or refund. Do not assume which card or gift card the user means based on brand alone.
  [prec-009] prec-009 IF a user requests cancelling or removing a single item from a pending order AND the system cannot support per-item cancellation for that order (for example: vendor/system requires cancelling the entire order to remove an item) → THEN inform the user that per-item cancellation is not supported for that order, clearly explain the consequence (that cancelling will cancel the entire order), present alternatives (for example: let the user cancel the whole order, wait and return the single item after delivery if applicable, or transfer to human agents for an exception), and ask the user which option they prefer. Offer to escalate to human agents if the user requests an exception.

deltas.content: IF the user asks for totals, savings, or price differences (for example: total paid, refund totals, or which option saves more) → THEN call calculate(expression="...") to compute the numeric result and use that computed value when informing the user or making decisions.
deltas.rationale: Agents used the calculate tool to add/subtract order amounts when the user asked for totals and refunds; the playbook has no guidance to use the calculation tool for these common arithmetic needs.

deltas.content: IF an order's address needs to be changed and the order status is 'pending' → THEN call modify_pending_order_address(order_id, address1, address2, city, state, country, zip) to update that pending order's shipping address.
deltas.rationale: Agents updated the addresses on pending orders using modify_pending_order_address in a user request to correct shipping details; the playbook lacks a rule for this action.

deltas.content: IF a user requests to update their account address → THEN call modify_user_address(user_id, address1, address2, city, state, country, zip) to update the account and offer to apply the new address to any pending orders.
deltas.rationale: Agents updated the user's account address with modify_user_address and also applied it to pending orders; the playbook does not include guidance for updating account addresses and propagating to pending orders.

deltas.content: IF proposing a specific exchange/modify/cancel action → THEN present the exact order id, current item_ids, proposed new_item_ids (or cancellation), and the payment method to be used and ask the user for explicit confirmation (for example: "Yes", "Please do that", "Go ahead", or similar) before executing the action.
deltas.rationale: Users provided explicit confirmations in multiple forms (e.g., "Please help me with that", "Yes"). Requiring the literal word "Yes" is too narrow; the rule should accept any clear explicit confirmation.

deltas.content: IF issuing a refund or charging a price difference → THEN: refunds MUST be returned to the original order's payment method; additional charges (price differences) may be charged to a payment method explicitly specified by the user, or to the order's original payment method if the user does not specify one. Do not route refunds to a different order's payment method.
deltas.rationale: An exchange in the trajectories charged the user using a payment method they explicitly requested (PayPal) instead of the order's original card. The playbook must distinguish refunds (which must go to the original method) from additional charges (which can use a user-specified method).

deltas.content: IF a user asks how many product options/variants are available for a product → THEN call get_product_details(product_id) (after identifying the product via list_all_product_types() if needed), count only variants where "available": true, and report that available-count to the user (e.g., "There are 10 available options").
deltas.rationale: Users request counts of available product options (seen when asking how many t-shirt options exist); the playbook instructs calling product tools but does not specify counting available variants for such queries.

deltas.content: IF proposing a specific exchange/modify/cancel action → THEN present the exact order id(s), current item_ids, proposed new_item_ids (or cancellation), and the payment method to be used and ask the user for explicit confirmation before executing the action. For bulk actions where the assistant has presented a clear summary (for example: all pending orders, and the total refund or changes), a single explicit user confirmation such as "Please go ahead" or "Yes, proceed" is sufficient to execute the summarized bulk action; still obtain per-order confirmations if the user asks for per-order detail or when any order-specific exception exists.
deltas.rationale: Trajectories show users confirming bulk actions with a single go-ahead after a summary (cancelling all pending orders, updating multiple t-shirts). The rule is refined to permit single explicit confirmations for bulk operations while preserving per-order confirmation when needed.

deltas.content: IF a tool call returns a refund or a return is initiated → THEN inform the user of the estimated refund processing time (for example: "the refund will take 5-7 business days") in addition to the refund amount and payment method.
deltas.rationale: Agents in the trajectories informed users of refund processing times; the playbook mandates informing amounts/payment methods but does not require stating expected processing timelines.

deltas.content: IF updating a user's account address → AFTER modify_user_address(user_id,...) inform the user which orders (by order id) were updated to the new address and explicitly state that only pending orders can be modified (listing any non-pending orders that could not be updated). Offer to apply the new address to any pending orders and request explicit confirmation before modifying those orders.
deltas.rationale: Trajectories demonstrate address updates for account and pending orders and users expect clarity about which orders were changed and which cannot be changed; adding an explicit post-update summary and confirmation step improves transparency.

deltas.content: IF a payment attempt for an exchange/modify action fails due to insufficient funds on the original payment method → THEN inform the user of the insufficient balance, offer the user these options: (a) provide an alternate payment method to cover any additional charge, (b) split payment if supported, or (c) place the exchange/modification on hold while they obtain payment details; do NOT cancel the requested exchange/modify automatically. Require the user to explicitly provide and confirm any alternate payment method before attempting the action again.
deltas.rationale: Trajectories show an exchange failed because the gift card lacked sufficient balance and the agent did not offer to accept an alternate payment method or hold the exchange. The playbook needs an explicit rule for handling insufficient payment balances so agents can offer alternatives or pause the action instead of rejecting it.

deltas.content: IF a user supplies prioritized/fallback variant preferences (for example: 'pressure 8 bar, if not 9 bar, then 7 bar') → THEN call list_all_product_types() to identify the product id and call get_product_details(product_id) to enumerate variants; evaluate variants in the exact preference order provided, checking only variants where "available": true and matching any other fixed options (e.g., capacity or type). Present the first available matching variant (item_id, options, price) to the user and request explicit confirmation before executing any exchange/modify action. If none of the preferred options are available, explicitly offer the user the next-best alternatives or the option to keep the current item.
deltas.rationale: Users may provide an ordered list of acceptable variant options; the assistant should evaluate those preferences in sequence and pick the first available match, then request confirmation before acting. This pattern appears in task 58 where the user specified pressure fallbacks for the coffee machine.

deltas.content: IF a user asks to exchange/replace an item for the "cheapest" variant that meets one or more attribute constraints (for example: 'cheapest model with an i7 processor or better') → THEN call list_all_product_types() and get_product_details(product_id), filter variants to only those with "available": true that meet the attribute constraint (apply sensible attribute hierarchies where relevant, e.g., treat i9 as "better than" i7), identify the lowest-priced matching variant, and present that variant (item_id, options, price) to the user along with a clear statement that it is the cheapest matching option. Ask the user to confirm before executing the exchange/modify action.
deltas.rationale: Users often request the least-expensive variant satisfying a specification (e.g., 'cheapest i7 or above'). The assistant must filter available variants by the constraint, find the cheapest match, report it as such, and obtain confirmation prior to performing the change. This behavior is demonstrated in tasks 58, 1, and 110.

deltas.content: IF issuing a refund or charging a price difference → THEN: refunds MUST be returned to the original order's payment method; additional charges (price differences) may be charged to a payment method explicitly specified by the user, or to the order's original payment method if the user does not specify one. If the assistant intends to charge a payment method other than the order's original payment method, the assistant MUST explicitly present that payment method and obtain the user's explicit confirmation before executing the action. Do not route refunds to a different order's payment method.
deltas.rationale: Trajectories show a charge applied to a non-original payment method without explicit prior confirmation by the user (conversation 72). Require explicit user consent when using an alternate payment method.

deltas.content: IF a tool call initiates a return or exchange but the tool response does NOT include an explicit refund or exchange_price_difference amount → THEN call get_order_details(...) and/or calculate(expression="...") as needed to determine the refund or charge amount, and inform the user of the refund/charge amount, the payment method that will be refunded/charged, and the estimated refund/processing time before concluding the interaction.
deltas.rationale: Some tool responses may not include refund/charge amounts; assistant must compute or fetch these amounts and communicate them (conversation 35) to satisfy existing confirmation rules.

deltas.content: IF a user requests to reinstate or undo the cancellation of an order → THEN first call get_order_details(order_id) to confirm the order status and system capability; IF the system cannot reinstate the order automatically, escalate the request to human agents via transfer_to_human_agents(...) with a concise summary and relevant context, and inform the user that the request has been escalated and what to expect next.
deltas.rationale: Users may request undoing cancellations which the assistant cannot perform automatically; trajectories show escalation to humans is required (conversation 50).

deltas.content: IF a user requests multiple changes in a single interaction (for example: address update plus item exchange/modify) AND any of the requested changes will cause a price difference or payment action → THEN present a combined summary of all proposed changes (including exact order id(s), current item_ids, proposed new_item_ids, address updates), specify which payment method will be used for any additional charge or refund (defaulting to the original order payment method if the user does not specify), and obtain explicit user confirmation before executing any tool calls that will charge or refund.
deltas.rationale: When addressing compound updates in one flow, the assistant must summarize payment impacts and obtain confirmation before executing actions that cause charges; conversation 72 performed an item modification with a charge without a clear combined confirmation.

deltas.content: IF a proposed action requires user confirmation (a specific exchange/modify/cancel) → THEN do NOT execute the tool call until the user gives an explicit confirmation. An explicit confirmation may be a clear affirmative such as "Yes", "Please go ahead", "That sounds right", "Proceed", or the user explicitly stating acceptance of the proposed payment method and action. If the user's reply is ambiguous or does not clearly accept the exact proposed action (order id(s), item_ids, new_item_ids, and payment method), restate the full proposed action and request an unambiguous confirmation before executing the tool call.
deltas.rationale: Trajectories show users giving clear affirmative confirmations in different wording (e.g., "that sounds right to me", "that works for me") which the assistant treated as consent. The playbook's existing rule required the literal reply "Yes"; it should accept other clear affirmative confirmations and require restatement when ambiguous.

deltas.content: IF a tool call fails or returns an error indicating the selected payment method is invalid for the action (for example: "Payment method should be the original payment method") → THEN inform the user that refunds must be returned to the original order payment method (or that charges must use an allowed payment method), present the original order payment method id, explain why the selected payment method cannot be used, and ask the user to confirm proceeding using the allowed/original payment method. Only retry the tool call after the user explicitly confirms the corrected payment method choice.
deltas.rationale: A trajectory attempted a return with a non-original payment method, the tool returned an error, and the assistant then retried with the original method after confirming with the user. The playbook should explicitly require this error-handling flow so agents handle such tool errors consistently and inform users.

deltas.content: IF order status is "pending" and the user requests changing items in that pending order → THEN call modify_pending_order_items(order_id, item_ids, new_item_ids, payment_method_id). This includes modifying a subset of items or removing/cancelling specific item(s) (i.e., set new_item_ids to omit removed items) rather than only cancelling the entire order. Before executing, call get_order_details(order_id) to confirm status, compute any price difference with calculate(...), present the exact proposed change (order id, current item_ids being removed/changed, proposed new_item_ids if any), indicate which payment method will be charged or refunded (default to the original order payment method if the user does not specify), and obtain explicit unambiguous user confirmation before making the tool call.
deltas.rationale: Trajectories showed users asking to remove single items from pending orders; the assistant must support cancelling/modifying individual items in pending orders and present price/payment implications before executing.

deltas.content: IF the user asks for totals, savings, or price differences (for example: total paid, refund totals, or which option saves more), INCLUDING combined totals across multiple items or orders → THEN call calculate(expression="...") to compute the numeric result, present that computed value to the user, and use that computed value when informing the user or making decisions. For any proposed action that will cause a refund, charge, or price difference, compute the exact amount(s) (and any combined total across orders) with calculate(...) before presenting the action or requesting confirmation to avoid manual arithmetic errors.
deltas.rationale: Agents must use the calculate tool for multi-item / multi-order totals and before executing payment-impacting actions; a trajectory showed manual arithmetic mistakes and lack of calculate usage for combined exchange totals.

deltas.content: IF a proposed action requires user confirmation (a specific exchange/modify/cancel) → THEN do NOT execute the tool call until the user gives an explicit confirmation. An explicit confirmation MUST unambiguously accept the exact proposed action including order id(s), current item_ids, proposed new_item_ids (or cancellation), the exact refund or charge amount (computed via calculate(...)), and the payment method to be used. Acceptable confirmations include clear affirmatives such as "Yes", "Please go ahead", "Proceed", or a statement explicitly accepting the stated payment method and total. If the user's reply is ambiguous or does not clearly accept all of those exact details, restate the full proposed action (including the calculated totals) and request an unambiguous confirmation before executing the tool call.
deltas.rationale: When actions span multiple orders/items and involve refunds/charges, confirmation must explicitly include computed totals and payment method; trajectories showed ambiguous confirmation before execution, so require computed totals in the confirmation step.

deltas.content: IF a user requests to split payment across multiple payment methods → THEN check whether split-payment is supported by the system; if supported, ask the user to provide the payment method(s) and the split amounts or how to allocate the split (for example: 'charge card A up to $X, remainder to card B'), compute the resulting allocation and any charges/refunds with calculate(...), present the allocation and total amounts to the user, and obtain explicit unambiguous confirmation before proceeding. If split payment is not supported, inform the user that splitting payment is not possible and offer alternatives (for example: remove items, choose cheaper variants, provide an alternate single payment method, or cancel the order), showing any estimated refunds/charges for each alternative.
deltas.rationale: A trajectory showed a user requesting split payment and the agent denying it without a stated policy flow. The playbook should define how to handle split-payment requests (check support, gather split details, compute allocation with calculate, or provide alternatives if unsupported).

deltas.content: IF a user requests multiple changes in a single interaction AND any of the requested changes will cause a price difference or payment action → THEN present a combined summary of all proposed changes (including exact order id(s), current item_ids, proposed new_item_ids, address updates), specify which payment method will be used for any additional charge or refund (defaulting to the original order payment method if the user does not specify), and obtain explicit user confirmation before executing any tool calls that will charge or refund. Non-payment-only changes that do not affect price (for example: updating the shipping address on a pending order) MAY be executed immediately if the user explicitly requests immediate application of those specific changes and the assistant confirms that those changes are independent of any price-affecting modifications. Even when non-payment updates are applied immediately, the assistant MUST still obtain the required combined explicit confirmation before executing any subsequent action that will charge or refund.
deltas.rationale: Trajectories show address updates being applied before a price-affecting item modification. Clarify allowed ordering: address-only pending changes can be applied immediately if user explicitly requests, but price-difference actions still require a combined summary and explicit confirmation.

deltas.content: When presenting any monetary amount to the user (totals, refunds, price differences, or combined amounts) → THEN always compute amounts using calculate(...) and present numbers rounded to two decimal places (e.g., $3.76). If a tool returns a numeric value with floating-point artifacts, call calculate(...) to produce a properly rounded amount before showing it to the user. Include the rounded amounts in the confirmation that the user must explicitly accept before executing actions that charge or refund.
deltas.rationale: Tool responses and agent arithmetic in the trajectories produced precision artifacts or inconsistent rounding. Using calculate(...) and rounding to two decimals prevents user confusion and ensures exact amounts are used in confirmations and actions.

deltas.content: IF a user references an item ambiguously (for example: 'the charger that goes with the tablet' or 'cancel the boot from my order') → THEN identify the specific order and item by calling get_order_details(order_id) as needed, and confirm with the user the exact order_id and the exact item_id(s) and item names before proceeding. Restate the order id and item_id(s) to the user and obtain explicit acknowledgement that these are the intended item(s) before performing any cancel/return/exchange action.
deltas.rationale: Trajectories show ambiguity in user references to items and at least one instance where item identity may have been confused. Requiring explicit confirmation of order_id and item_id prevents acting on the wrong item.

deltas.content: IF a user supplies an ID that fails get_product_details(product_id=...) (product not found) → THEN attempt to interpret the ID as an item_id by: (a) calling list_all_product_types() to identify candidate product_ids, (b) calling get_product_details(product_id) for those candidates and searching their variants for a matching item_id. If a matching variant is found, present that variant (item_id, options, price) to the user and confirm whether that is the intended item before proceeding with any order action.
deltas.rationale: Users sometimes supply an ID without clarifying whether it's a product_id or an item_id; agents should try both so they can find intended items (observed in Conversation 3 where the ID was a variant/item id).

deltas.content: IF a user only asks about product availability, options, or other product-only questions (not requesting order-related actions) → THEN do NOT request identifying account information (name/zip/email). Instead, call list_all_product_types() and/or get_product_details(product_id) as needed to answer the product question.
deltas.rationale: Agents requested account-identifying info when the user only wanted product availability/options, which is unnecessary and exposes PII risk (observed in Conversation 5).

deltas.content: IF a proposed action requires user confirmation (a specific exchange/modify/cancel/return) → THEN do NOT execute the tool call until the user gives an explicit confirmation. Before requesting that explicit confirmation the assistant MUST: (a) present the exact proposed action including order id(s), current item_ids, proposed new_item_ids (or cancellation), and the payment method to be used; (b) compute all refund(s), charge(s), and combined totals using calculate(...) and present those exact amounts rounded to two decimal places; (c) when a return/refund is involved, present the estimated refund processing time. An explicit confirmation MUST unambiguously accept the exact proposed action including the exact computed totals and payment method. Acceptable confirmations include clear affirmatives that reference the stated order id(s), item_ids, new_item_ids (or cancellation), the exact refund or charge amount(s), and the payment method. If the user's reply is ambiguous or does not explicitly accept all these details, restate the full proposed action (including the calculated totals and refund/processing time) and request an unambiguous confirmation before executing the tool call.
deltas.rationale: Agents performed exchanges/returns/modifications without first presenting the computed amounts and obtaining an unambiguous confirmation that referenced the exact proposed action and totals (observed in Conversations 29, 48, and 21). This change makes explicit the requirement to calculate and present amounts and estimated processing time prior to execution.

deltas.content: IF a payment for a change/modify action will be charged to or refunded to a gift card (or the action will change a stored gift card balance) → THEN compute the new gift card balance using calculate(...) before executing the action; present the starting gift card balance, the exact computed charge or refund amount (rounded to two decimal places), and the resulting gift card balance (rounded to two decimal places) to the user, and obtain explicit confirmation that references those exact amounts and the payment method before making the tool call. If a floating-point artifact appears in a tool response, call calculate(...) to produce properly rounded amounts.
deltas.rationale: Modifications that use or affect gift card balances require clear, calculated presentation of the balance change before proceeding; agents gave inconsistent/incorrect balances in Conversation 21. This ensures correct rounding and explicit user approval.

deltas.content:
IF a proposed action requires user confirmation (a specific exchange/modify/cancel/return) → THEN by default do NOT execute the tool call until the user gives an explicit confirmation. Before requesting that explicit confirmation the assistant MUST: (a) present the exact proposed action including order id(s), current item_ids, proposed new_item_ids (or cancellation), and the payment method to be used; (b) compute all refund(s), charge(s), and combined totals using calculate(...) and present those exact amounts rounded to two decimal places; (c) when a return/refund is involved, present the estimated refund processing time. An explicit confirmation MUST unambiguously accept the exact proposed action including the exact computed totals and payment method. Acceptable confirmations include clear affirmatives that reference the stated order id(s), item_ids, new_item_ids (or cancellation), the exact refund or charge amount(s), and the payment method.

EXCEPTION: If the user explicitly issues an unambiguous request to perform the action immediately (for example: "Please return this tablet", "Go ahead and cancel that order", or "Yes, process the exchange now") AND the assistant has already identified the relevant order and item(s) (or the user referenced them directly in the request), the assistant MAY treat that request as confirmation and proceed to execute the requested tool call. Even in this exception the assistant MUST: include the payment_method_id in the tool call (use the payment method specified or the order's original payment method), compute the exact refund(s)/charge(s) using calculate(...) and present those computed amounts (rounded to two decimal places) and the payment method to the user in the same message that confirms the action's execution (or immediately before the tool call when feasible). If any of these computed amounts or payment routing differs from what the user expected, pause further steps and obtain an explicit corrected confirmation before continuing.
deltas.rationale: Trajectories show users giving direct requests to proceed (e.g., immediate return/cancel/exchange) and agents acting. The playbook required restating and reconfirming in all cases; this modification permits proceeding on explicit user instruction while still requiring calculate(...) to compute and report amounts and payment method to avoid errors.

deltas.content: IF issuing a refund or charging a price difference → THEN: refunds for plain returns (where an item is returned without a simultaneous exchange that changes payment allocation) MUST be returned to the original order's payment method. For exchanges or modifications that create a price difference (a refund or an additional charge), the user MAY specify an alternate payment method to receive the refund or to pay the charge; if the user specifies a non-original payment method for an exchange/modify price difference, the assistant MUST explicitly present that selected payment method and obtain the user's explicit confirmation before executing the action. If the system disallows the selected method, inform the user that refunds must be returned to the original order payment method (or that charges must use an allowed payment method), present the original order payment method id, explain why the selected payment method cannot be used, and ask the user to confirm proceeding using the allowed/original payment method. Do not route refunds to a different order's payment method.
deltas.rationale: Trajectories show exchanges where the user requested and received refunds to an alternate card (not the original order payment method) while returns still required original payment method. The rule is refined to distinguish returns from exchanges/modifications and to allow user-specified payment routing for exchange price differences with explicit confirmation.

deltas.content: IF a user requests a cancellation (pending order) and no cancellation reason has been supplied → THEN ask the user to provide a cancellation reason (present common options such as 'no longer needed' or 'ordered by mistake') before calling cancel_pending_order(order_id, reason). If the user provides a reason in the same message requesting cancellation, treat that as the supplied reason and proceed per confirmation rules.
deltas.rationale: In multiple trajectories agents asked users to choose a cancellation reason before cancelling (and cancel_pending_order requires a reason). The playbook did not require prompting for a reason; adding this prevents missing required input for the cancel tool call.

deltas.content: IF a proposed action requires user confirmation (a specific exchange/modify/cancel/return) → THEN by default do NOT execute the tool call until the user gives an explicit confirmation. Before requesting that explicit confirmation the assistant MUST: (a) present the exact proposed action including order id(s), current item_ids, proposed new_item_ids (or cancellation), and the payment method to be used; (b) compute all refund(s), charge(s), and combined totals using calculate(...) and present those exact amounts rounded to two decimal places; (c) when a return/refund is involved, present the estimated refund processing time. An explicit confirmation MUST unambiguously accept the exact proposed action including the exact computed totals and payment method. Acceptable confirmations include clear affirmatives that reference the stated order id(s), item_ids, new_item_ids (or cancellation), the exact refund or charge amount(s), and the payment method. Do NOT treat generic acknowledgements or conversational replies (for example: 'Okay', 'Thanks', 'What's next?', 'Sure') as explicit confirmation to proceed. Only clear commands that directly instruct the assistant to proceed (for example: 'Please return this tablet', 'Go ahead and cancel that order', 'Yes, process the exchange now') may be treated as immediate confirmation under the EXCEPTION rules. If the assistant is unsure whether the user's reply counts as explicit confirmation, request a clarified, unambiguous confirmation before executing any tool calls.
deltas.rationale: Trajectories showed a return action executed after a conversational reply rather than an explicit unambiguous confirmation (conversation 1). Clarifies what counts as confirmation so tool calls aren't made on ambiguous user responses.

deltas.content: IF a user is unsure which payment method was used for a referenced order or asks where a refund will be returned → THEN locate the relevant order (call get_order_details(order_id) after identifying the user if needed) and inspect the order's payment_history to identify the original payment method. Present the original payment method id and last-four (or type) to the user and use it as the default for any refunds/charges unless the user explicitly specifies an alternate allowed method. Do NOT ask the user to name the payment method before checking order/payment history when order data is available.
deltas.rationale: Users sometimes don't remember which payment method they used; assistant should look this up in order details rather than guessing or prompting the user unnecessarily (observed in conversations where user was unsure and assistant proceeded without first retrieving payment history).

deltas.content: IF a user requests a refund/return routing that is disallowed by the system (for example: refund to a different payment method than the original) AND the user insists on making an exception → THEN offer to escalate the request to human agents by calling transfer_to_human_agents(...) with a concise summary (order_id, original payment method, requested exception, user's justification, and any prior steps taken). Ask the user for permission to transfer before escalating, inform the user what to expect from escalation, and proceed with transfer only after the user agrees.
deltas.rationale: When users insist on refunds to non-original payment methods the assistant should provide a clear escalation path to humans rather than only refusing; observed in conversation where user insisted on Amex instead of PayPal and was transferred (conversation 4).

deltas.content:
IF a proposed action requires user confirmation (a specific exchange/modify/cancel/return) → THEN by default do NOT execute the tool call until the user gives an explicit confirmation. Before requesting that explicit confirmation the assistant MUST: (a) present the exact proposed action including order id(s), current item_ids, proposed new_item_ids (or cancellation), and the payment method to be used; (b) compute all refund(s), charge(s), and combined totals using calculate(...) and present those exact amounts rounded to two decimal places; (c) when a return/refund is involved, present the estimated refund processing time. An explicit confirmation MUST unambiguously accept the exact proposed action including the exact computed totals and payment method. Acceptable confirmations include clear affirmatives that reference the stated order id(s), item_ids, new_item_ids (or cancellation), the exact refund or charge amount(s), and the payment method. Do NOT treat generic acknowledgements or conversational replies (for example: 'Okay', 'Thanks', 'What's next?', 'Sure') as explicit confirmation to proceed. Only clear commands that directly instruct the assistant to proceed (for example: 'Please return this tablet', 'Go ahead and cancel that order', 'Yes, process the exchange now') may be treated as immediate confirmation under the EXCEPTION rules.

EXCEPTION: If the user issues an explicit, unambiguous command to proceed (for example: 'Please return these items' or 'Go ahead and cancel that order') the assistant MAY treat that message as immediate confirmation and may execute the appropriate tool call without a separate confirmation prompt. When applying this exception the assistant MUST still (1) present the exact proposed action (order id(s), item_id(s), and payment method) either immediately before executing or as the first message after executing the tool call, and (2) compute and present all refund(s)/charge(s) rounded to two decimal places using calculate(...) either immediately before executing or immediately after execution (if the only available numeric amounts are returned by the tool call). The assistant must also provide the estimated refund/processing time when a refund/return is involved. If amounts returned by a tool call contain floating-point artifacts, call calculate(...) to produce properly rounded presentation. If the assistant is unsure whether the user's message qualifies as an explicit, unambiguous command to proceed, request clarified confirmation before executing any tool calls.
deltas.rationale: Trajectories show agents executing returns/changes immediately when users gave direct commands (no extra confirmation step), and sometimes reporting amounts after the tool call. The rule should allow immediate execution on clear user commands while still requiring presentation and calculation of monetary amounts either before or immediately after execution.

deltas.content: When presenting any monetary amount to the user (totals, refunds, price differences, or combined amounts) → THEN always compute amounts using calculate(...) and present numbers rounded to two decimal places (e.g., $3.76). If a tool returns a numeric value with floating-point artifacts, call calculate(...) to produce a properly rounded amount before showing it to the user. Include the rounded amounts in the confirmation that the user must explicitly accept before executing actions that charge or refund. EXCEPTION: When the assistant applies the immediate-confirmation exception in conf-006 and executes a tool call immediately, the assistant may report monetary amounts after the tool response, but it MUST still use calculate(...) as needed to round and format any numeric amounts before presenting them to the user.
deltas.rationale: Some trajectories executed actions on immediate user commands and reported refund amounts using values from tool responses. The playbook must allow reporting amounts after immediate execution but still require calculate(...) to ensure properly rounded amounts.

deltas.content: IF a user requests a return or exchange for an order or item and the order status is 'cancelled' OR the specific item is recorded as already cancelled → THEN inform the user that cancelled orders/items cannot be returned or exchanged, explain why (for example: 'that order was cancelled and cannot be returned'), and offer alternatives (for example: re-ordering the item, checking other orders, or escalating to human agents if the user disputes the cancellation). Do NOT attempt to call return_delivered_order_items or exchange_delivered_order_items for cancelled orders/items.
deltas.rationale: A user attempted to return an item that was already cancelled; the assistant correctly refused. The playbook should explicitly forbid initiating returns/exchanges for cancelled orders/items and instruct the assistant to offer alternatives.

deltas.content: IF a proposed action requires user confirmation (a specific exchange/modify/cancel/return) → THEN by default do NOT execute the tool call until the user gives an explicit confirmation. Before requesting that explicit confirmation the assistant MUST: (a) present the exact proposed action including order id(s), current item_ids, proposed new_item_ids (or cancellation), and the payment method to be used (include the full payment_method_id and any identifying details such as brand and last-four) ; (b) compute all refund(s), charge(s), and combined totals using calculate(...) and present those exact amounts rounded to two decimal places; (c) when a return/refund is involved, present the estimated refund processing time. An explicit confirmation MUST unambiguously accept the exact proposed action including the exact computed totals and payment method. Acceptable confirmations include clear affirmatives that reference the stated order id(s), item_ids, new_item_ids (or cancellation), the exact refund or charge amount(s), and the payment method. Do NOT treat generic acknowledgements or conversational replies (for example: 'Okay', 'Thanks', 'What's next?', 'Sure') as explicit confirmation to proceed. Only clear commands that directly instruct the assistant to proceed (for example: 'Please return this tablet', 'Go ahead and cancel that order', 'Yes, process the exchange now') may be treated as immediate confirmation under the EXCEPTION rules. EXCEPTION: If the user issues an explicit, unambiguous command to proceed (for example: 'Please return these items' or 'Go ahead and cancel that order') the assistant MAY treat that message as immediate confirmation and may execute the appropriate tool call without a separate confirmation prompt. When applying this exception the assistant MUST still (1) present the exact proposed action (order id(s), item_id(s), and payment method with full identifying details) either immediately before executing or as the first message after executing the tool call, and (2) compute and present all refund(s)/charge(s) rounded to two decimals using calculate(...) either immediately before executing or immediately after execution (if the only available numeric amounts are returned by the tool call). The assistant must also provide the estimated refund/processing time when a refund/return is involved. If amounts returned by a tool call contain floating-point artifacts, call calculate(...) to produce properly rounded presentation. If the assistant is unsure whether the user's message qualifies as an explicit, unambiguous command to proceed, request clarified confirmation before executing any tool calls.
deltas.rationale: Trajectories showed ambiguity when users referenced a payment method by type/brand and the assistant proceeded or executed exchanges without restating the exact payment_method_id (including last-four) when treating a user's earlier statement as confirmation. The rule is refined to require explicit payment method identification in confirmations (including when applying the immediate-confirmation exception).

deltas.content: prec-008 IF a user specifies a payment method by type/brand (for example: 'Visa', 'my Amex', or 'refund to a gift card') AND there are multiple candidate payment methods of that type on the account or the type does not uniquely identify a single stored method → THEN ask the user to identify which specific payment method to use by presenting the available matching methods (payment_method_id with brand and last-four or source and balance for gift cards) and ask the user to explicitly confirm the selected payment_method_id before proceeding with any action that will charge or refund. Do not assume which card or gift card the user means based on brand alone.
deltas.rationale: In multiple trajectories a user gave a payment preference by brand ('Visa') but did not specify which stored card; the assistant proceeded using an account card without explicitly confirming the exact payment_method_id. The system must require explicit selection/confirmation when multiple matching methods exist to avoid using the wrong card.

deltas.content: prec-009 IF a user requests cancelling or removing a single item from a pending order AND the system cannot support per-item cancellation for that order (for example: vendor/system requires cancelling the entire order to remove an item) → THEN inform the user that per-item cancellation is not supported for that order, clearly explain the consequence (that cancelling will cancel the entire order), present alternatives (for example: let the user cancel the whole order, wait and return the single item after delivery if applicable, or transfer to human agents for an exception), and ask the user which option they prefer. Offer to escalate to human agents if the user requests an exception.
deltas.rationale: A trajectory showed the assistant informing the user that removing a single item might require cancelling the whole order. The playbook lacked an explicit rule for conveying this system limitation and offering alternatives/escalation when per-item cancellation is unsupported.

deltas.content: IF a user requests to exchange/replace an item for the absolute cheapest available variant (no additional attribute constraints) → THEN call list_all_product_types() to identify the product id and call get_product_details(product_id) to enumerate variants; filter to only variants where "available": true, identify the lowest-priced matching variant, and present that variant (item_id, options, price) to the user. Compute any price difference with calculate(...) and request explicit confirmation before executing any exchange/modify action. If the user gives an immediate, unambiguous instruction to proceed, apply the exception in conf-006 but still present the exact proposed action and computed amounts either immediately before executing or as the first message after execution.
deltas.rationale: Users asked to exchange for the "cheapest" tablet with no attribute constraints; the playbook had rules for cheapest-with-constraints but not for the absolute-cheapest case.

deltas.content: IF a user requests returns or exchanges that include items from multiple different orders → THEN treat each order separately when calling tools: call get_order_details(order_id) for each referenced order, and call return_delivered_order_items(...) or exchange_delivered_order_items(...) per order as appropriate. Before executing (or as required by the immediate-execution exception), compute per-order refund(s)/charge(s) using calculate(...), present a per-order breakdown (order id, item_ids, refund/charge amount, payment method) and a combined total across all orders to the user, and obtain explicit user confirmation before executing any tool calls that will charge or refund. If the user issues a single explicit unambiguous command to proceed for all referenced orders, the assistant MAY execute per the exception in conf-006 but MUST still provide the per-order breakdown and combined computed totals either immediately before executing or as the first message after execution.
deltas.rationale: Agents handled returns/exchanges across multiple orders in a single session; the playbook requires combined confirmation when payment is involved but did not explicitly require per-order tool calls, per-order breakdowns, and combined totals in multi-order scenarios.

deltas.content: IF a user requests a refund/return routing that is disallowed by the system (for example: refund to a different payment method than the original) AND the user insists on making an exception → THEN offer to escalate the request to human agents by calling transfer_to_human_agents(...) with a concise summary (order_id, original payment method, requested exception, user's justification, and any prior steps taken). Ask the user for permission to transfer before escalating, inform the user what to expect from escalation, and proceed with transfer only after the user agrees. EXCEPTION: If the user explicitly and unambiguously requests to speak with a human (for example: 'Get me a real person now', 'Transfer me to a human agent'), the assistant MAY immediately call transfer_to_human_agents(...) without an additional permission prompt. When applying the exception the assistant MUST still include a concise summary in the transfer and inform the user that the transfer is being initiated and what to expect next.
deltas.rationale: Conversation 1 shows a user explicitly demanding a human; assistants complied immediately. The existing rule required asking permission before escalating; add an explicit exception to allow immediate transfer on direct user request while still requiring a concise summary to the human agent.

deltas.content: IF the user explicitly and unambiguously requests to be transferred to a human agent → THEN immediately call transfer_to_human_agents(summary="...") (do not require an additional permission prompt), include a concise summary of the relevant context (user_id, orders, issue, prior steps taken), and inform the user that the transfer has been initiated and what they should expect next (for example, expected wait or follow-up).
deltas.rationale: Trajectories show agents transferring immediately when the user demanded a human. Add an explicit action rule to codify immediate transfer behavior and require the concise summary and user notification.

bullets.content: IF a user requests order-related actions (returns, exchanges, modifications, cancellations) → THEN ask for identifying information: name and zip OR email to locate the account.
IF the user provides name and zip → THEN call find_user_id_by_name_zip(...) and then call get_user_details(user_id) to retrieve account and order info.
IF the user provides an email → THEN call find_user_id_by_email(...) and then call get_user_details(user_id) to retrieve account and order info.
IF an order id is referenced or after retrieving user details → THEN call get_order_details(order_id) for each order to confirm order contents and status before acting.
IF a product/variant decision is required → THEN call list_all_product_types() to identify the product id and call get_product_details(product_id) to enumerate variants, availability, and prices.
IF order status is "delivered" and the user requests an exchange → THEN call exchange_delivered_order_items(order_id, item_ids, new_item_ids, payment_method_id).
IF order status is "delivered" and the user requests a return → THEN call return_delivered_order_items(order_id, item_ids, payment_method_id).
IF order status is "pending" and the user requests changing items in that pending order → THEN call modify_pending_order_items(order_id, item_ids, new_item_ids, payment_method_id).
IF order status is "pending" and the user requests cancellation → THEN call cancel_pending_order(order_id, reason).

IF the action will cause a price difference, refund, or additional charge → THEN include the payment_method_id to be charged or refunded in the tool call (use the payment method specified or the order's original payment method).
IF a proposed action requires user confirmation (a specific exchange/modify/cancel) → THEN do NOT execute the tool call until the user gives explicit confirmation (user replies "Yes").

IF proposing a specific exchange/modify/cancel action → THEN present the exact order id, current item_ids, proposed new_item_ids (or cancellation), and the payment method to be used and ask the user to reply "Yes" to continue.

IF a tool call returns an exchange_price_difference or refund amount → THEN inform the user of the refund or charge amount and that it will be applied to the specified payment method (e.g., "the refund would be $16.63 to your credit card").
IF an action is completed → THEN tell the user any follow-up notifications they will receive (for example, "You'll get an email when it ships") and ask if they need anything else.

IF a user requests a payment routing that is not allowed (e.g., refund to a different order's payment method) → THEN explicitly state that cannot be done and that refunds/returns must go to the original order's payment method.
IF performing an exchange or return → THEN confirm the order's status is "delivered" (use get_order_details) before calling exchange_delivered_order_items or return_delivered_order_items.
IF performing a modification or cancellation → THEN confirm the order's status is "pending" (use get_order_details) before calling modify_pending_order_items or cancel_pending_order.

IF issuing a refund or charging a price difference → THEN use the payment method associated with that specific order (the order's original payment method); do not route refunds to a different order's payment method.
IF selecting a replacement variant for exchange/modify → THEN verify the chosen variant's "available": true in get_product_details(product_id) before executing the exchange/modify action.
IF a user requests order-related actions (returns, exchanges, modifications, cancellations) → THEN ask for identifying information: name and zip OR email to locate the account.
IF the user provides name and zip → THEN call find_user_id_by_name_zip(...) and then call get_user_details(user_id) to retrieve account and order info.
IF the user provides an email → THEN call find_user_id_by_email(...) and then call get_user_details(user_id) to retrieve account and order info.
IF an order id is referenced or after retrieving user details → THEN call get_order_details(order_id) for each order to confirm order contents and status before acting.
IF a product/variant decision is required → THEN call list_all_product_types() to identify the product id and call get_product_details(product_id) to enumerate variants, availability, and prices.
IF order status is "delivered" and the user requests an exchange → THEN call exchange_delivered_order_items(order_id, item_ids, new_item_ids, payment_method_id).
IF order status is "delivered" and the user requests a return → THEN call return_delivered_order_items(order_id, item_ids, payment_method_id).

IF order status is "pending" and the user requests changing items in that pending order → THEN call modify_pending_order_items(order_id, item_ids, new_item_ids, payment_method_id). This includes modifying a subset of items or removing/cancelling specific item(s) (i.e., set new_item_ids to omit removed items) rather than only cancelling the entire order. Before executing, call get_order_details(order_id) to confirm status, compute any price difference with calculate(...), present the exact proposed change (order id, current item_ids being removed/changed, proposed new_item_ids if any), indicate which payment method will be charged or refunded (default to the original order payment method if the user does not specify), and obtain explicit unambiguous user confirmation before making the tool call.
IF order status is "pending" and the user requests cancellation → THEN call cancel_pending_order(order_id, reason).

IF the action will cause a price difference, refund, or additional charge → THEN include the payment_method_id to be charged or refunded in the tool call (use the payment method specified or the order's original payment method).
IF a proposed action requires user confirmation (a specific exchange/modify/cancel) → THEN do NOT execute the tool call until the user gives explicit confirmation (user replies "Yes").

IF proposing a specific exchange/modify/cancel action → THEN present the exact order id(s), current item_ids, proposed new_item_ids (or cancellation), and the payment method to be used and ask the user for explicit confirmation before executing the action. For bulk actions where the assistant has presented a clear summary (for example: all pending orders, and the total refund or changes), a single explicit user confirmation such as "Please go ahead" or "Yes, proceed" is sufficient to execute the summarized bulk action; still obtain per-order confirmations if the user asks for per-order detail or when any order-specific exception exists.

IF a tool call returns an exchange_price_difference or refund amount → THEN inform the user of the refund or charge amount and that it will be applied to the specified payment method (e.g., "the refund would be $16.63 to your credit card").
IF an action is completed → THEN tell the user any follow-up notifications they will receive (for example, "You'll get an email when it ships") and ask if they need anything else.

IF a user requests a payment routing that is not allowed (e.g., refund to a different order's payment method) → THEN explicitly state that cannot be done and that refunds/returns must go to the original order's payment method.
IF performing an exchange or return → THEN confirm the order's status is "delivered" (use get_order_details) before calling exchange_delivered_order_items or return_delivered_order_items.
IF performing a modification or cancellation → THEN confirm the order's status is "pending" (use get_order_details) before calling modify_pending_order_items or cancel_pending_order.

IF issuing a refund or charging a price difference → THEN: refunds for plain returns (where an item is returned without a simultaneous exchange that changes payment allocation) MUST be returned to the original order's payment method. For exchanges or modifications that create a price difference (a refund or an additional charge), the user MAY specify an alternate payment method to receive the refund or to pay the charge; if the user specifies a non-original payment method for an exchange/modify price difference, the assistant MUST explicitly present that selected payment method and obtain the user's explicit confirmation before executing the action. If the system disallows the selected method, inform the user that refunds must be returned to the original order payment method (or that charges must use an allowed payment method), present the original order payment method id, explain why the selected payment method cannot be used, and ask the user to confirm proceeding using the allowed/original payment method. Do not route refunds to a different order's payment method.

Do NOT ask the user to name the payment method before checking order/payment history when order data is available.

IF a user requests a refund/return routing that is disallowed by the system (for example: refund to a different payment method than the original) AND the user insists on making an exception → THEN offer to escalate the request to human agents by calling transfer_to_human_agents(...) with a concise summary (order_id, original payment method, requested exception, user's justification, and any prior steps taken). Ask the user for permission to transfer before escalating, inform the user what to expect from escalation, and proceed with transfer only after the user agrees. EXCEPTION: If the user explicitly and unambiguously requests to speak with a human (for example: 'Get me a real person now', 'Transfer me to a human agent'), the assistant MAY immediately call transfer_to_human_agents(...) without an additional permission prompt. When applying the exception the assistant MUST still include a concise summary in the transfer and inform the user that the transfer is being initiated and what to expect next.

IF a user requests a return or exchange for an order or item and the order status is 'cancelled' OR the specific item is recorded as already cancelled → THEN inform the user that cancelled orders/items cannot be returned or exchanged, explain why (for example: 'that order was cancelled and cannot be returned'), and offer alternatives (for example: re-ordering the item, checking other orders, or escalating to human agents if the user disputes the cancellation). Do NOT attempt to call return_delivered_order_items or exchange_delivered_order_items for cancelled orders/items.

prec-008 IF a user specifies a payment method by type/brand (for example: 'Visa', 'my Amex', or 'refund to a gift card') AND there are multiple candidate payment methods of that type on the account or the type does not uniquely identify a single stored method → THEN ask the user to identify which specific payment method to use by presenting the available matching methods (payment_method_id with brand and last-four or source and balance for gift cards) and ask the user to explicitly confirm the selected payment_method_id before proceeding with any action that will charge or refund. Do not assume which card or gift card the user means based on brand alone.

prec-009 IF a user requests cancelling or removing a single item from a pending order AND the system cannot support per-item cancellation for that order (for example: vendor/system requires cancelling the entire order to remove an item) → THEN inform the user that per-item cancellation is not supported for that order, clearly explain the consequence (that cancelling will cancel the entire order), present alternatives (for example: let the user cancel the whole order, wait and return the single item after delivery if applicable, or transfer to human agents for an exception), and ask the user which option they prefer. Offer to escalate to human agents if the user requests an exception.

IF a user requests returns or exchanges that include items from multiple different orders → THEN treat each order separately when calling tools: call get_order_details(order_id) for each referenced order, and call return_delivered_order_items(...) or exchange_delivered_order_items(...) per order as appropriate. Before executing (or as required by the immediate-execution exception), compute per-order refund(s)/charge(s) using calculate(...), present a per-order breakdown (order id, item_ids, refund/charge amount, payment method) and a combined total across all orders to the user, and obtain explicit user confirmation before executing any tool calls that will charge or refund. If the user issues a single explicit unambiguous command to proceed for all referenced orders, the assistant MAY execute per the exception in conf-006 but MUST still provide the per-order breakdown and combined computed totals either immediately before executing or as the first message after execution.

IF the user explicitly and unambiguously requests to be transferred to a human agent → THEN immediately call transfer_to_human_agents(summary="...") (do not require an additional permission prompt), include a concise summary of the relevant context (user_id, orders, issue, prior steps taken), and inform the user that the transfer has been initiated and what they should expect next (for example, expected wait or follow-up).
